mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at the zero lower bound (0.39%) represents extreme monetary accommodation which compresses discount rates on equity cash flows, making stocks more attractive relative to bonds, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns negative over 60 days in >50% of zero lower bound periods, or rank IC for FEDFUNDS level < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, it signals severe labor market slack that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings, historically leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances after UNRATE crosses above 6.5%, or rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate synchronized demand-supply contraction, which depresses corporate profitability and equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and RSAFS show month-over-month decline
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative in periods of simultaneous declines (t-test p > 0.05), or signal produces positive returns in >55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low housing starts (<1000k units) in a recession signal that the housing market is near a trough, historically preceding a cyclical recovery and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days, but the direction flips to negative in expansionary regimes when low starts indicate weakness.
regime_hint: recession with housing market near trough
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1000 during recession, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in >60% of episodes, or during expansions the hypothesized negative direction fails to hold (returns positive > 60%).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or below 0.5% indicates the central bank has cut rates to emergency levels, which historically triggers a risk-on rally as markets anticipate further easing or economic stabilization, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in >50% of instances following FEDFUNDS falling below 0.5%, or rank IC of binary trigger is < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Low inflation, as measured by the CPI index, allows the Federal Reserve to maintain an accommodative monetary stance, reducing real interest rates and stimulating equity demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL as a continuous predictor over 60-day forward returns is < 0.02, or positive returns are not significantly higher in low inflation periods (>2% trim mean).

mechanism_narrative: Large simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (>100k) and retail sales (>1%) reveal severe labor market weakening that directly reduces consumer spending, causing sharp short-term equity selloffs over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS change < -100k MoM and RSAFS change < -1% MoM
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >40% of triggered instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP (negative growth) during a deep recession is a lagging indicator that, once confirmed, often marks the point of maximum pessimism, leading to a mean-reverting rally and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; the signal flips to negative in late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: During deep recession (as defined by NBER), negative GDP growth is followed by 60-day S&P 500 losses in >50% of cases, or in late-cycle expansions the hypothesized negative direction fails to produce losses > 55% of the time.
